title: P0 CDK: port network_construct.py (VPC + SGs + endpoints)
description: Port deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/network_construct.py, adapted for RouteIQ's single stateless pod. VPC routeiq:vpc_cidr default 10.40.0.0/16 (distinct from VSR 10.20/10.30), max_azs=2, nat_gateways=1 (keyword-only, default floor), enable_dns_hostnames + enable_dns_support ON. Three subnet tiers: public (PUBLIC), private-app (PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS — pod ENIs + interface endpoints), private-data (PRIVATE_ISOLATED — reserved for P1). Tag public kubernetes.io/role/elb=1, private kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb=1. SGs two-phase (instantiate ALL first, wire ingress after — avoids CFN SG-to-SG circular dependency): alb_sg (443 from any_ipv4, narrowable), pod_sg (uvicorn port 4000 from alb_sg — NOT VSR Envoy 8080/8700), vpce_sg (443 from pod_sg, shared by all interface endpoints). DROP efs_sg/milvus_sg/backend_vllm_sg (stateless). Every SG description in the EC2 charset allowlist (no arrows/em-dashes/backticks — mulch ec2-sg-description-charset). Endpoints (P0-minimum 6 + S3 gateway): ECR, ECR_DOCKER, CLOUDWATCH_LOGS, SECRETS_MANAGER, BEDROCK_RUNTIME (needs aws-cdk-lib>=2.150.0), + S3 gateway endpoint (route-table assoc on both private tiers, free, no SG). DROP STS from P0-required (was IRSA-only); DROP SagemakerRuntime + the sr.internal Cloud Map namespace (VSR-specific). All interface endpoints share vpce_sg, land in private-app, private_dns_enabled=True. Add VPC flow logs (ALL) for AwsSolutions-VPC7. Re-read the real vllm-sr source before copying (discover docs are reconstructions). Refs: discover-network.md; outline section 8.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py exists exposing self.vpc, self.public_subnets, self.private_app_subnets, self.private_data_subnets, the 3 SGs (alb_sg/pod_sg/vpce_sg), self.interface_endpoints (6 entries incl BedrockRuntime), and self.s3_gateway_endpoint. uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit asserts via has_resource_properties/find_resources that the VPC CIDR is 10.40.0.0/16, exactly the 6 interface endpoints + S3 gateway exist, BEDROCK_RUNTIME endpoint is present, pod_sg ingress is TCP 4000 from alb_sg, and no STS/Sagemaker/Cloud-Map resources. All SG descriptions pass the EC2 charset allowlist. Synth is byte-stable across flag-off runs.

title: P0 CDK: port eks_cluster_construct.py with EKS Pod Identity (NOT IRSA)
description: Port deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, but BAKE IN THE POD IDENTITY DECISION: use EKS Pod Identity, NOT IRSA, per the research verdict (research/notes/final_report_eks-automode-cdk-irsa-podidentity-9dc4a2.md, very high confidence; AWS names Pod Identity recommended for Auto Mode; AWS docs state the eks-pod-identity-agent is built into Auto Mode, BUT the production VSR Auto Mode CDK installs eks-pod-identity-agent explicitly via eks.CfnAddon (vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py, comment: 'must be present for Pod Identity associations to resolve') — resolve empirically before relying on zero addon cost, and add the addon defensively (see the CfnAddon directive below); the L1 CfnCluster constraint strips L2 addServiceAccount which would hide IRSA's complexity, so Pod Identity is strictly simpler on L1). L1 eks.CfnCluster, version 1.33, with the 3 Auto-Mode blocks enabled: compute_config (node_pools=[general-purpose, system], node_role_arn), storage_config.block_storage, kubernetes_network_config.elastic_load_balancing. bootstrap_self_managed_addons=False; access_config authentication_mode=API + bootstrap_cluster_creator_admin_permissions=True. Hand-build the two Auto Mode IAM roles (AmazonEKSAutoClusterRole + AmazonEKSAutoNodeRole; node role carries AmazonEKSWorkerNodeMinimalPolicy + AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryPullOnly). CfnAccessEntry for the node role (type EC2, NO access policies — AWS API constraint) and the operator/CI kubectl identity (routeiq:admin_principal_arns). REPLACE the IRSA factory with a pod_identity_association(self, cid, *, namespace, service_account, role) helper emitting eks.CfnPodIdentityAssociation (clusterName/namespace/serviceAccount/roleArn) — DELETE OpenIdConnectProvider, oidc_provider_issuer, CfnJson trust map, and the WebIdentityPrincipal (none needed; static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust). DEFENSIVE ADDON (resolves the pre-installed-vs-installed contradiction): emit a cheap, idempotent eks.CfnAddon(addon_name='eks-pod-identity-agent', resolve_conflicts='OVERWRITE') depending on the L1 CfnCluster, BEFORE the association, and make the CfnPodIdentityAssociation depend_on this addon — so the association is guaranteed to resolve regardless of whether Auto Mode pre-installs the agent. This mirrors what the production VSR construct does (eks_cluster_construct.py:391-398). The OVERWRITE conflict mode makes re-applying a built-in addon a harmless no-op; build wave should verify empirically (aws eks list-addons) but ship the addon either way. enable_container_insights(): amazon-cloudwatch-observability addon + CDK-created routing log group (MUST pre-exist before the MetricFilter or first deploy fails) SHIP at P0; the routing_latency_ms_by_model MetricFilter ships PREP-ONLY/deferred to P2 (data-source-blocked — its source, the structured routing_decision CW JSON log line, is itself a P2 BUILD-NEW item, roadmap:124-129; src today emits an OTel span/logger.info event, observability.py:784, not a CW JSON line, so the filter matches nothing until P2). When it does ship, its dimension key MUST be dimensions={model: $.["gen_ai.response.model"]} per ADR-0027:64-67 — RouteIQ emits gen_ai.response.model (telemetry_contracts.py:673); selected_model is NEVER emitted, so a $.selected_model filter matches ZERO events. Keep the addon + log group at P0; only the dimensioned filter is data-source-blocked. defer the 7 alarms/dashboard/SNS to P2. CfnOutputs: ClusterName, ClusterEndpoint, NodeRoleName (DROP OidcProviderArn/IssuerUrl — no OIDC on the Pod Identity path). File the ADR-0030 amendment (it currently assumes IRSA). Re-read real vllm-sr source first. Refs: discover-eks-cluster.md; research report; outline sections 3, 7.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py emits an AWS::EKS::Cluster with the 3 Auto-Mode blocks enabled (compute/storage/elastic-load-balancing), bootstrap_self_managed_addons=False, authentication_mode=API. The pod_identity_association() helper emits AWS::EKS::PodIdentityAssociation with a static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust. A defensive AWS::EKS::Addon eks-pod-identity-agent (ResolveConflicts=OVERWRITE, DependsOn the cluster) is emitted, and the AWS::EKS::PodIdentityAssociation DependsOn that addon, so the association resolves regardless of whether Auto Mode pre-installs the agent (test asserts the addon + the DependsOn edge exist). The synthesized template contains NO Custom::AWSCDKCfnJson and NO AWS::IAM::OIDCProvider (proves Pod Identity, not IRSA). Node CfnAccessEntry is type EC2 with no access policies. enable_container_insights() creates the addon + a CDK log group at P0; the routing_latency_ms_by_model MetricFilter is PREP-ONLY/deferred to P2 (data-source-blocked — the structured routing_decision CW JSON log line is a P2 BUILD-NEW item, roadmap:124-129; src emits an OTel/logger.info event, observability.py:784, not a CW JSON line) and, when shipped, dimensions on $.["gen_ai.response.model"] (NOT $.selected_model, which is never emitted) per ADR-0027:64-67 / telemetry_contracts.py:673. All IAM role Descriptions are ASCII/Latin-1. ADR-0030 amended to record the Pod-Identity-over-IRSA decision with the research cite.
dependsOnTitles: P0 CDK: port network_construct.py (VPC + SGs + endpoints)

title: P0 CDK: port ecr_construct.py (GHCR pull-through cache + RepositoryCreationTemplate governance on the cached repo)
description: Port deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, parameterized DOWN from VSR's 7 repos to RouteIQ's single gateway repo + the GHCR pull-through cache rule. GOVERNANCE TARGET CORRECTION: the immutable-tag + scan-on-push payoff applies to the repo the PTC rule AUTO-CREATES on first pull (<acct>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/ghcr/baladithyab/routeiq), NOT a standalone ecr.Repository (nobody pulls from a standalone repo, so governance on it is unobserved; ECR's default for an un-templated PTC repo is tag-MUTABLE, no scan). DROP the standalone ecr.Repository at P0 (it is not the pull target). Express the governance via a CfnRepositoryCreationTemplate (ecr_construct.py ~:145-160 in VSR): prefix='ghcr' (MUST equal the PTC rule's EcrRepositoryPrefix), applied_for=['PULL_THROUGH_CACHE'], image_tag_mutability='IMMUTABLE', DependsOn the PTC rule. Tag immutability IS a template prop; scan-on-push is NOT (AWS::ECR::RepositoryCreationTemplate has no ImageScanningConfiguration field) — deliver scan-on-push on the cached copy via a registry-level AWS::ECR::RegistryScanningConfiguration (ScanFrequency=SCAN_ON_PUSH, RepositoryFilter ghcr/*). NOTE: PTC-cached repos cannot be made immutable retroactively — the template MUST exist before the first pull (settings apply only at repo-creation time); provision it in the same cdk deploy as the PTC rule, ahead of any chart deploy. AWS recommends MUTABLE for PTC templates so same-tag images can refresh; IMMUTABLE is acceptable here because the chart pins by image.digest + immutable release tags. GHCR pull-through cache (load-bearing): L1 ecr.CfnPullThroughCacheRule with ecr_repository_prefix=ghcr, upstream_registry=github-container-registry, upstream_registry_url=ghcr.io, credential_arn=<ecr-pullthroughcache/* secret ARN passed as a param>. The credential secret is OPERATOR-PROVISIONED out-of-band, NOT created by CDK (holds a real GitHub PAT — no secrets in source): name prefix ecr-pullthroughcache/ (CFN pattern enforced), same account+region, MUST use the AWS-managed aws/secretsmanager key (ECR does NOT support a CMK for the PTC credential secret — hard constraint), contents {username, accessToken} with PAT scope read:packages. IAM split: the CDK/CFN exec role gets ecr:CreatePullThroughCacheRule (identity-based) + secretsmanager:GetSecretValue/DescribeSecret on the PTC secret; the NODE/pull identity gets ecr:BatchImportUpstreamImage + the normal pull set — do NOT put these on the application pod role. enable_ghcr_ptc flag (routeiq: context). Zero image-pipeline rework: CI keeps pushing to ghcr.io/baladithyab/routeiq; only the chart image.repository repoints to <acct>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/ghcr/baladithyab/routeiq:<tag>. Per-region caveat: PTC rules are per-Region, CredentialArn updates require replacement. Re-read real vllm-sr source first. Refs: discover-ecr-ghcr.md; outline section 9.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py emits one AWS::ECR::PullThroughCacheRule with UpstreamRegistryUrl=ghcr.io, UpstreamRegistry=github-container-registry, EcrRepositoryPrefix=ghcr, and CredentialArn taken from the construct param (never a literal secret); one AWS::ECR::RepositoryCreationTemplate with AppliedFor=['PULL_THROUGH_CACHE'], Prefix='ghcr', ImageTagMutability='IMMUTABLE' (DependsOn the PTC rule); and a registry scan-on-push for ghcr/* via AWS::ECR::RegistryScanningConfiguration (ScanFrequency=SCAN_ON_PUSH, RepositoryFilter ghcr/*). The construct emits NO standalone AWS::ECR::Repository (the standalone repo is dropped — it is not the PTC pull target). uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit/test_ecr.py asserts: the PullThroughCacheRule props; the RepositoryCreationTemplate exists with AppliedFor=PULL_THROUGH_CACHE + Prefix=ghcr + ImageTagMutability=IMMUTABLE; the registry SCAN_ON_PUSH rule on ghcr/*; and that NO AWS::ECR::Repository resource is present. The construct does NOT create the credential secret. No application-pod-role ecr:* grants are emitted by this construct.

title: P0 CDK: RouteIqStack wiring + app.py + cdk.json routeiq: context keys (ONE stack)
description: Build the composition root deploy/cdk/lib/routeiq_stack.py + deploy/cdk/app.py + deploy/cdk/cdk.json + requirements.txt + README.md. ONE stack for P0 (single stateless pod, one VPC/cluster/ECR/pod-role) — Aurora is P1 and gets a SEPARATE stack/CI-stage per the ~30-min-rollback rule, so the split does NOT happen at P0. app.py copies the VSR helper SHAPE verbatim: _ctx, _bool_ctx (defuses bool('false')==True from --context string), _split_csv_or_list; builds cdk.App, reads routeiq:* context, builds cdk.Environment from CDK_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT/REGION, constructs RouteIqStack(app, f'RouteIqStack-{env}', env=…, **flags), then cdk.Aspects.of(app).add(AwsSolutionsChecks(verbose=True)) and app.synth(). RouteIqStack instantiates in order: NetworkConstruct -> EksClusterConstruct(vpc=...) -> EcrConstruct -> the ONE pod role (with the P0 grant set, see separate seed if split, else inline: Bedrock invoke + SecretsManager:GetSecretValue + S3:GetObject + Logs put on the routing log group; static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust + sts:AssumeRole/sts:TagSession; DROP sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity — that was IRSA-only; ECR pull perms live on the NODE role, not this role) -> pod_identity_association(namespace=routeiq:sa_namespace, service_account=routeiq:sa_name, role=pod_role) -> CfnOutputs -> self._suppress_nag() LAST. Tags.of(self).add('routeiq:env', env_name) + conditional cost tags only when supplied (keeps default synth byte-stable). CfnOutputs: ClusterName, ClusterEndpoint, PodRoleArn, PodAssociationId, NodeRoleName, EcrGhcrPrefix. cdk.json: 'app':'python3 app.py' + flat context with routeiq: keys: env(dev), vpc_cidr(10.40.0.0/16), nat_gateways(1), k8s_version(1.33), enable_ghcr_ptc(true), sa_namespace, sa_name, image_tag, admin_principal_arns, bedrock_model_arns, config_s3_bucket, secret_arns. requirements.txt pins: aws-cdk-lib>=2.150.0,<3.0.0; cdk-nag>=2.27.0,<3.0.0; constructs>=10.0.0,<11.0.0; pytest>=7.4.0,<9.0.0. DROP cdklabs.ecs-codedeploy (VSR ECS-only); cdk-monitoring-constructs optional. Add a 'cdk' extra to root pyproject.toml (uv sync --extra cdk) as the uv-native path. Refs: discover-scaffold-tests.md; outline sections 4, 5, 6, 10.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/cdk/ tree exists with app.py (the 3 helpers + AwsSolutionsChecks aspect added before synth), cdk.json (app=python3 app.py + routeiq:* context defaults incl vpc_cidr=10.40.0.0/16 and sa_namespace/sa_name), requirements.txt with exactly the 4 pins (no ecs-codedeploy), and lib/routeiq_stack.py wiring NetworkConstruct + EksClusterConstruct + EcrConstruct + the one pod role + the pod_identity_association + the 6 CfnOutputs + self._suppress_nag() last. uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit/test_routeiq_stack.py asserts the pod role has exactly the P0 grant set (Bedrock invoke + SecretsManager + S3 + Logs), the static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust, NO sts:AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity, the CfnOutputs exist, and VPC CIDR is 10.40.0.0/16. Default synth is byte-stable.
dependsOnTitles: P0 CDK: port network_construct.py (VPC + SGs + endpoints)
P0 CDK: port eks_cluster_construct.py with EKS Pod Identity (NOT IRSA)
P0 CDK: port ecr_construct.py (GHCR pull-through cache + RepositoryCreationTemplate governance on the cached repo)

title: P0 chart: add loadBalancerSourceRanges + Pod Identity SA binding + AWS_REGION + EcrGhcrPrefix image override
description: Close the chart seams the CDK foundation requires (deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/). GAP-1 (genuine chart gap): under EKS Auto Mode managed load balancing the NLB CIDR allowlist is honored via spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges, NOT the legacy service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-internal annotation. Add (1) service.loadBalancerSourceRanges: [] to values.yaml and (2) render spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges in templates/service.yaml when type==LoadBalancer (guard like the existing nodePort block). Without this an Auto-Mode LoadBalancer Service is open to 0.0.0.0/0. Pod Identity SA binding (the flip from IRSA): the chart's ServiceAccount needs a STABLE (namespace, name) that the CDK CfnPodIdentityAssociation pins — set serviceAccount.create: true and an explicit serviceAccount.name (or document the rendered fullname), and that (namespace, name) pair is passed to the CDK as routeiq:sa_namespace/routeiq:sa_name. DO NOT add the eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn SA annotation (that is IRSA-only; Pod Identity binds CDK-side). This removes the discover-chart-seams GAP-2 apply-k-clobbers-annotation hazard for the IAM path and GAP-3's IRSA-token-automount assumption — retract that guidance. AWS_REGION: the chart emits no AWS_REGION env, so a Bedrock/AMP client with no region fails — add extraEnv: [{name: AWS_REGION, value: <region>}]. Leave networkPolicy.enabled: false at P0; flag that enabling it later needs explicit egress.to entries for in-VPC services + a PrivateLink Bedrock endpoint. GAP-4 (REQUIRED deploy-time image-repository override — this is the step that actually exercises the GHCR pull-through cache from the ECR construct): the chart ships image.repository=ghcr.io/baladithyab/routeiq (values.yaml:33) and global.imagePullSecrets=[] (values.yaml:18), so an unchanged helm upgrade pulls straight from ghcr.io and the pull-through cache is NEVER populated (the cache construct is provisioned but dead). Add the explicit operator deploy step (NOT a values.yaml default flip — keep the default ghcr.io so the chart stays account/region-agnostic and the snapshot baseline bakes in no account id): at deploy time repoint image.repository to <EcrGhcrPrefix>/baladithyab/routeiq via helm --set image.repository=<prefix>/baladithyab/routeiq, sourcing <prefix> from the EcrGhcrPrefix CfnOutput (aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RouteIqStack-<env> --query Outputs[?OutputKey=='EcrGhcrPrefix'].OutputValue) which equals <acct>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/ghcr. The EcrGhcrPrefix CfnOutput is the source of truth for the prefix. Document this in deploy/cdk/README.md's operator-deploy section (alongside aws eks update-kubeconfig + helm upgrade) and the chart deploy docs. Refs: discover-chart-seams.md (GAP-1/2/3, section 3); discover-ecr-ghcr.md section 4; proposal sections 9.4, 11.6; outline section 11.
acceptanceCriteria: P0 EDGE DECISION: loadBalancerSourceRanges ships INERT-UNTIL-LoadBalancer and the public edge is explicitly DEFERRED — values.yaml stays service.type: ClusterIP (verified default at deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/values.yaml:69) and ingress.enabled: false (:82), so the Auto-Mode managed LB does NOT render by default and alb_sg + loadBalancerSourceRanges have no live consumer at P0; the public edge attaches only when an operator later flips service.type to LoadBalancer. The conditional render guard stays AS-IS. helm template (or a chart unit test) renders spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges from values.service.loadBalancerSourceRanges when service.type==LoadBalancer and omits it otherwise (so with the ClusterIP default it emits nothing). values.yaml has service.loadBalancerSourceRanges: [] (inert default) and an AWS_REGION extraEnv entry (or documented operator override). serviceAccount.create=true with a stable name; NO eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn annotation is templated. The (sa_namespace, sa_name) pair is documented as the CDK routeiq:sa_namespace/sa_name contract. networkPolicy.enabled remains false with a documented note about egress.to for in-VPC + PrivateLink Bedrock. The edge-deferral (alb_sg + loadBalancerSourceRanges are PREP-ONLY, flag-gated, attach when the operator flips service.type) is documented in proposal §11.1a. The image.repository deploy-time override is a documented operator step (in deploy/cdk/README.md operator-deploy section + chart deploy docs) that repoints image.repository to <EcrGhcrPrefix>/baladithyab/routeiq at helm upgrade time, with the EcrGhcrPrefix CfnOutput named as the source of truth; values.yaml's image.repository default stays ghcr.io/baladithyab/routeiq (no default flip). Optionally a helm template / chart unit test asserts --set image.repository=<prefix>/... flows through to the rendered Deployment image.
dependsOnTitles: P0 CDK: port eks_cluster_construct.py with EKS Pod Identity (NOT IRSA)

title: P0 CDK: cdk-nag evidenced suppressions (nag_suppressions.py)
description: Build deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py copying only the SHAPE of the VSR file (NOT its 2400 lines). apply_nag_suppressions(stack) fans out to _suppress_<construct>(stack) helpers; each calls NagSuppressions.add_resource_suppressions_by_path(stack, '/<stack_id>/<Construct>/<Resource>/Resource', [NagPackSuppression(id, reason)]). Flag-gated families guarded with getattr(stack, 'x', None) because add_*_by_path RAISES on an absent path under cdk-nag>=2.27. Evidenced-suppression shape (the only thing to copy): every suppression carries an id (AwsSolutions-IAM4/-IAM5/-EKS1/-VPC3/...), an explicit appliesTo list (specific managed-policy ARNs or Resource::*), and a reason stating (a) WHY it's safe, (b) that it's least-privilege / the only valid form, (c) an Owner: line. RouteIQ-specific reasons — do NOT reuse VSR's. Enumerate the expected P0 suppressions: the AWS-managed Auto Mode policies on the cluster/node roles (IAM4 — 'Auto Mode REQUIRES these exact AWS-published policies, not replaceable with a narrower custom policy. Owner: EksClusterConstruct'); bedrock:InvokeModel on foundation-model/* (IAM5 — 'model set is deploy-time dynamic; scoped to the bedrock service + region. Owner: RouteIqStack'); the public 443 ALB ingress if flagged; ECR/log wildcards as needed. Called LAST in the RouteIqStack ctor via self._suppress_nag(). Refs: discover-scaffold-tests.md section 1.5; outline section 13.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py exposes apply_nag_suppressions(stack) + per-construct helpers with getattr guards. Every NagPackSuppression has a non-empty id, an explicit appliesTo, and a reason containing a why-safe clause, a least-privilege/only-valid-form clause, and an Owner: line. After self._suppress_nag(), uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit/test_cdk_nag.py reports zero surviving AwsSolutions-* errors and zero CdkNagValidationFailure at both the default and maximal flag surface.
dependsOnTitles: P0 CDK: RouteIqStack wiring + app.py + cdk.json routeiq: context keys (ONE stack)

title: P0 CDK: cred-free pytest synth + nag + snapshot test suite
description: Build deploy/cdk/tests/ (unit + snapshot) running fully offline. The gate command is `uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/` — every test calls Template.from_stack(stack) with an explicit dummy env=cdk.Environment(account='123456789012', region='us-west-2'); no AWS creds, no cdk CLI, no network. Wire into the pre-push hook / CI. test_eks_cluster.py: has_resource_properties('AWS::EKS::Cluster', ...) for the 3 Auto-Mode blocks enabled; node CfnAccessEntry type EC2; a CfnPodIdentityAssociation exists with the right namespace/serviceAccount; the IAM-role-Description-is-ASCII guard (em-dash passes synth but FAILS IAM CREATE); flag-on/off byte-stability. NEW negative guard (replaces VSR's https-strip test): assert the template contains NO Custom::AWSCDKCfnJson and NO AWS::IAM::OIDCProvider — the regression guard for the Pod-Identity-over-IRSA decision. Use find_resources/Match.object_like over brittle full counts (mulch cdk-resource-count-test-tripwire). test_ecr.py: GHCR CfnPullThroughCacheRule (upstream_registry_url=ghcr.io) + the CfnRepositoryCreationTemplate (AppliedFor=['PULL_THROUGH_CACHE'], Prefix='ghcr', ImageTagMutability='IMMUTABLE' — the immutable-tag governance on the PTC-cached ghcr/* repo) + the registry scan-on-push for ghcr/* (AWS::ECR::RegistryScanningConfiguration, ScanFrequency=SCAN_ON_PUSH) + assert NO standalone AWS::ECR::Repository is emitted (the standalone repo was dropped; it was never the pull target). test_routeiq_stack.py: pod role has exactly the P0 grant set (Bedrock invoke + Secrets + S3 + Logs), the static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust, CfnOutputs exist, VPC CIDR 10.40.0.0/16. test_cdk_nag.py: _synth_with_nag(**flags) adds AwsSolutionsChecks, asserts NO AwsSolutions-* errors AND no CdkNagValidationFailure.* at default + maximal flag surface; failure messages render the offending id + data[:200]. snapshot/test_template_snapshot.py: _synth_template -> to_json -> _canonicalise (sort_keys, indent=2) -> _assert_snapshot vs __snapshots__/dev.json; auto-write ONLY under UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1; a missing baseline is a LOUD failure (no silent create — false-green hazard); an autouse fixture monkeypatches Docker-bundled-asset path globals to a non-existent dir so synth stays hermetic. Commit the flag-off dev.json baseline once. Refs: discover-scaffold-tests.md sections 1.6, 3; outline section 12.
acceptanceCriteria: `uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/` passes with zero AWS creds and no network. The suite includes the NO-Custom::AWSCDKCfnJson + NO-AWS::IAM::OIDCProvider negative guard, the ASCII-IAM-description guard, the CfnPodIdentityAssociation presence assertion, the GHCR PullThroughCacheRule assertion, the RepositoryCreationTemplate assertion (AppliedFor=PULL_THROUGH_CACHE + Prefix=ghcr + ImageTagMutability=IMMUTABLE) plus the ghcr/* registry SCAN_ON_PUSH assertion and the NO-standalone-AWS::ECR::Repository guard, the pod-role P0-grant-set assertion, the cdk-nag zero-error gate at default + max flag surface, and the snapshot diff against __snapshots__/dev.json. A missing snapshot baseline fails loudly (never silently creates). The committed dev.json baseline exists. The gate is wired into the pre-push hook/CI.
dependsOnTitles: P0 CDK: RouteIqStack wiring + app.py + cdk.json routeiq: context keys (ONE stack)
